title: Mellow Sweet Tunisian Banana Tagine
description: A comforting and savory Tunisian dish featuring fried ripe bananas and nuka-pickle, perfect for a sick day. This recipe is FODMAP-friendly and offers a unique blend of umami flavors.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced bananas and cook until they are golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Add the chopped nuka-pickle, garlic-infused oil, ground cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the tagine simmer, covered, for 10 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Let the dish rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Reheat the tagine gently if serving later.
Serve with crusty bread or over rice or couscous.
